body.title: Granny: auto-reset orphaned in_progress tickets
body.result: OrphanWatchdog in devices/scraps/jobs/; Granny calls it every 10 poll cycles; S=120m M=240m L=360m XL=480m timeouts; 15 tests
body.description:
When a CC session dies without closing a ticket (crash, timeout, OOM), the ticket stays in_progress forever with no worker behind it. Currently requires manual `setstatus sprint`. Granny is the orchestrator — she should detect and recover these automatically.

Proposed shape: in GrannyDaemon.run_once(), after the dispatch loop, iterate all in_progress tickets. For each, check: (1) is there a live process matching `claude.*sprint-ticket.*<ticket_id>` in cmdline? Use `ps aux` via subprocess. (2) Has `claimed_at` exceeded the size-keyed timeout (S=2h, M=4h, L=6h, XL=8h)? If no live process AND timeout exceeded, call `cc_queue.py setstatus <id> sprint` and post `GRANNY_ORPHAN_RESET|ticket=<id>|age_minutes=<n>|reason=no_live_session` to the granny-weatherwax channel.

Initial timeouts are fixed defaults (S=2h etc.). T-ticket-build-time will add calibration later.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; ps check must grep cmdline for ticket ID to avoid false matches on unrelated claude sessions
**Scope boundary:** detection + reset only; no re-dispatch (Granny's normal poll cycle handles that on next tick); no change to claimed_at semantics
**Completion criteria:** kill a CC session mid-sprint without closing the ticket; Granny resets it to sprint within 2h (or configured timeout); GRANNY_ORPHAN_RESET appears in channel log
